articles.title: Lebanon death toll rises to 2,727 as Israeli raids continue
articles.summary: Lebanon death toll rises to 2,727 as Israeli raids continue The Lebanese Ministry of Public Health says the number of people killed in Israel’s war on Lebanon since March 2 has reached 2,727. In a statement, the ministry’s emergency operations centre said 8,438 people have been wounded as Israeli attacks continue across wide areas of the country. Officials said the strikes remain concentrated in towns, villages and cities in southern Lebanon, where bombardment has intensified.

articles.title: Jordan tourism sector faces downturn as visitor numbers plunge amid Iran war
articles.summary: Jordan tourism sector faces downturn as visitor numbers plunge amid Iran war Tourism, which accounts for around 14 percent of Jordan’s economy, is facing a sharp downturn as the Iran war disrupts travel across the region. Official figures show the sector supports about 60,000 direct jobs, with a further 300,000 livelihoods linked to it. Last year, more than seven million visitors generated $7.8bn in revenue. articles.title: 'Shutdown': Moody's expects Dubai hotel occupancy to plummet to 10 percent
articles.summary: 'Shutdown': Moody's expects Dubai hotel occupancy to plummet to 10 percent The US-based financial analysis firm Moody's predicts that hotel occupancy in Dubai is set to plummet to 10 percent from a pre-war figure of 80 percent in the second quarter of the year, which ends on 1 July, The Wall Street Journal reported on Wednesday. "This represents an effective shutdown of large parts of the hospitality sector," Moody’s said. articles.title: Iran says ceasefire and ending war top priorities in talks
articles.summary: Iran says ceasefire and ending war top priorities in talks Iran’s foreign ministry spokesperson, Esmaeil Baghaei, says current discussions centre on securing a ceasefire, ending the war and restoring regional stability. In an interview with IRNA, Baghaei said officials in Tehran are still examining the US proposal and will decide once the review is complete. He added that Iran will relay its decision to Pakistani mediators, with the next steps to depend on the outcome of the ongoing exchange of messages.
